parameters.name: order
parameters.label: Order
parameters.description: Determines the order of the integral to use. If the input is a velocity, a First Order integral will return the position. If the input is an acceleration, a Second Order integral will return the posit

parameters.name: speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: Allows generating values when no inputs are connected. This parameter is disabled as soon as an input is connected to the Speed .

parameters.name: constant1
parameters.label: First Constant
parameters.description: Constant to add to the entire result after integrating once.

parameters.name: constant2
parameters.label: Second Constant
parameters.description: Constant to add to the entire result after integrating twice.

parameters.name: constant3
parameters.label: Third Constant
parameters.description: Constant to add to the entire result after integrating three times.

parameters.name: limittype
parameters.label: Limit Type
parameters.description: Select limit options such as loop, clamp, or zigzag from the menu. The value will remain in the range from Min to less than Max.

parameters.name: min
parameters.label: Minimum
parameters.description: The minimum value the output channel can have.

parameters.name: max
parameters.label: Maximum
parameters.description: The maximum value the output channel can have.

parameters.name: speedsamples
parameters.label: Speed per Sample
parameters.description: Applies the speed to each sample of the channel instead of across the whole channel. Useful for working with multi-sample channels.

parameters.name: resetcondition
parameters.label: Reset Condition
parameters.description: This menu determines how the Reset input triggers a reset of the channel(s).

parameters.name: resetvalue
parameters.label: Reset Value
parameters.description: The channel(s) is set to this value when reset.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: While On, this toggle resets the channel(s) to the .

parameters.name: resetonstart
parameters.label: Reset on Start
parameters.description: While On, the Speed will reset each time the file is restarted. If the Speed 's value gets too large it can start stepping as when it reaches the limit of 's number resolution. This is useful for proj

parameters.name: resetpulse
parameters.label: Reset Pulse
parameters.description: Click to instantly resets the channel(s) to the .

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

examples.label: Configure Time Slice and Reset on Start
examples.code:
n = op('speed1')
n.par.timeslice = True
n.par.resetonstart = False
n.par.max = 3
examples.description: Common settings from 43 real Derivative examples

examples.label: Alternative configuration
examples.code:
n = op('speed1')
n.par.timeslice = True
n.par.resetonstart = False
